title: Indexing in the Vector DB
concept: Writing chunk vectors and source page metadata fields into the vector database. 'Next up: LAE Project: Retrieval & QA.'.

exercises.prompt: What do we call the dense representations of text chunks that help us in semantic search?
exercises.explain: Chunk vectors are the dense representations created from text chunks to assist in searching.

cards.title: What is Indexing?
cards.body: Indexing is like creating a library catalog. It helps organize information so you can quickly find what you need.

cards.title: Indexing in Vector DB
cards.body: In a vector database, indexing involves writing chunk vectors and their related metadata so you can retrieve them later.

cards.title: Indexing Explained
cards.body: Think of indexing like filing documents in a filing cabinet. Each document needs a tag (metadata) and a location (vector) to find it easily.

cards.title: Example of Chunk Vector Indexing
cards.body: When you have a chunk vector like [0.23, -0.15, 0.75], you also assign it metadata like 'page 3, paragraph 2' for easy retrieval.

cards.title: Myth: Indexing is Just for Text
cards.body: Indexing can also apply to images or sound clips. It’s a general method for organizing any type of data.

cards.title: Visualizing Indexing
cards.body: Indexing consists of two parts: the chunk vector and the metadata.

exercises.prompt: What information do you need to store in a vector database?
exercises.explain: You need to store both chunk vectors for semantic search and their metadata for context.

exercises.prompt: Indexing in a vector database only requires the chunk vector and not the metadata.
exercises.explain: False. You need both chunk vectors and metadata to retrieve and understand the context of the information.

exercises.prompt: To retrieve information efficiently, we store chunk vectors along with their _____ in the vector database.
exercises.sentence: To retrieve information efficiently, we store chunk vectors along with their metadata in the vector database.
exercises.explain: Metadata is essential for understanding the context of the chunk vectors.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes the data stored in a vector database?

options.text: Chunk vectors and metadata for retrieval

exercises.explain: Chunk vectors and metadata are stored together to enhance efficiency and retrieve relevant information.

exercises.prompt: What is the importance of storing both chunk vectors and metadata in a vector database for your project?

options.text: It helps in making food recipes
It allows for efficient information retrieval and context understanding
It is optional; only chunk vectors are needed

exercises.explain: Storing both chunk vectors and metadata allows for efficient retrieval of information and understanding the context, making your PDF Q&A bot effective. Next up: LAE Project: Retrieval & QA.
